Point clouds are unordered without regular structures, making it impossible to directly transfer networks from the 2D image to the 3D point cloud.
Qi et al. solve the problem of unordered point clouds by designing a T-Net network by directly inputting the point cloud.
Velickovic et al. propose a graph attention network that computes the weighting coefﬁcients of points and selectively focuses on the most relevant neighborhood features of point clouds.
Wang et al. design an EdgeConv module based on the topology of the graph to get the edge features of neighborhoods points, maintaining alignment invariance.
Wu et al. encode the point cloud into a series of parallel sequences and extract features based on a shared recurrent neural network.
Zhang et al. develop a new convolutional method (EAGConv) by combining the advantages of feature alignment invariance in DGCNN and computational efﬁciency in PointNet.
